user_query:
I’m a fellow customer who values how clearly Santander International explains things. Please help me plan a small, trust-building editorial calendar that stays grounded in official information.

Use the config in input/pillars.yaml as the single source of truth for pillars, keywords, required fields, and output paths. Then:

1) Discover and collect sources (Internet required):
- Use a search engine to find official Santander International pages for each pillar defined in input/pillars.yaml.
- Only use pages whose hostnames contain the allowed_host_substring from the config (no third‑party blogs or news sites).
- Find at least the configured min_pages_per_pillar relevant to each pillar’s keywords.
- Keep a simple plaintext log of the exact search queries you use at the path specified in the config (search_queries_log_path).

2) Download and extract structured data:
- For each selected page, download the HTML and save a local copy in the raw HTML directory defined in input/pillars.yaml (one file per URL; sanitize the filename using hostname and path).
- Extract the following for each page: url, page_title, meta_description, h1, h2s (list), detected_pillar (map by matching the config keywords), matched_keywords (list of pillar keywords found), and retrieved_at (ISO timestamp).
- Save the full list of extracted page objects to the pages JSON path from the config.

3) Build the editorial calendar using the sources:
- Create at least the configured min_ideas per pillar.
- For each calendar row (idea), populate all fields listed in calendar_required_fields in input/pillars.yaml. Ensure:
  • pillar_id matches one of the configured pillars.
  • primary_source_url is one of the collected pages for that pillar.
  • supporting_source_urls includes at least one other collected page (use semicolon-separated URLs).
  • rationale references which headings or keywords from the sources support the idea and explains how it builds trust/clarity.
  • cta_copy is written in the tone described in input/style_guide.md.
- Save the CSV to the calendar path from the config.

4) Verification report:
- Create a concise report at the report path from the config that lists, per pillar: number of pages collected, number of ideas produced, whether all minimums were met, and any items that failed validation (e.g., wrong domain or missing fields).
- Include a domain compliance checklist and a brief note confirming you followed input/style_guide.md.
- Add the footer note from input/style_guide.md verbatim.

Important:
- Do not include personal data; only use publicly available official pages matching the allowed host substring.
- Adhere to the tone and CTA guidance in input/style_guide.md.
- Use the exact output paths provided in input/pillars.yaml:
  • pages JSON path
  • raw HTML directory for downloaded pages
  • calendar CSV path
  • verification report path
  • search queries log path

Deliverables (must exist locally after completion):
- Pages JSON with the required fields extracted from official pages.
- Raw HTML files for each source page.
- Editorial calendar CSV with at least the configured minimum ideas per pillar.
- Verification report documenting counts and checks.
- Search queries log.

Inputs:
- input/pillars.yaml (config and schema)
- input/style_guide.md (tone and CTA guidance)

Outputs (as per config):
- output/sources/pages.json
- output/sources/raw/… (one HTML file per page)
- output/calendar/calendar.csv
- output/reports/verification.md
- output/sources/search_queries.txt All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
